title: foeniculum vulgare subsp. subinodorum
intro: Foeniculum vulgare subsp. subinodorum, also known as wild fennel, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is closely related to the common fennel (Foeniculum vulgare). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Foeniculum vulgare subsp. subinodorum include its ability to relieve digestive problems, alleviate respiratory issues, and reduce menstrual cramps, among others. Fennel seeds and leaves can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to cooking and used in traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties.
